error: Quality gate failed: deterministic physics violations (11)

task.task_description: Design and simulate a silicon pixel tracking detector system for charged particle momentum measurement through Monte Carlo simulation. This is a detector design task that involves creating a multi-layer silicon sensor configuration optimized for particle tracking while maintaining minimal material budget. The task requires testing different geometric configurations using Geant4 simulations with muon beams to evaluate detector performance metrics including energy deposition patterns, hit detection efficiency, and material budget contributions across multiple energy points.
task.goal: Achieve good momentum resolution for charged particles while minimizing material budget in a silicon pixel tracking detector

constraints.description: Total material budget < 0.1 X₀ per layer
Silicon layer thickness between 100-500 μm (Geant4 minimum: 100 μm)
Energy sweep capped at 30 GeV and 3 points
Absorber should be zero or minimal (air gaps)
Detector must be transparent to particles

task.context:
Design a silicon pixel tracking detector for measuring charged particle momenta.

The tracker must achieve good momentum resolution while minimizing material budget.

**GEOMETRY MODES:**
- topology="tracking_detector": Thin sensor layers with air gaps — designed for trackers.
  Requires: active_material (Silicon), active_thickness (meters, e.g., 0.0003 for 300μm),
  num_layers, transverse_size (meters), layer_gap (meters, e.g., 0.02 for 2cm air gap).
  No absorber needed — absorber is automatically set to air.
- topology="cylinder_barrel": Concentric cylindrical shells — for barrel trackers.
  Can also use with absorber_material="Air" and thin silicon active layers.
- custom_spec: Full flexibility with solids, volumes, placements.

TRACKER PHYSICS:
- A tracker should be TRANSPARENT to particles — total material budget < 0.1 X₀ per layer
- Use thin silicon layers (100-500 μm) as active material (Geant4 minimum: 100 μm = 0.1 mm)
- Absorber should be ZERO or minimal (air gaps between layers) — NOT iron or lead
- The goal is position measurement, NOT energy containment

PRACTICAL CONSTRAINTS (for tractable simulation):
- Energy sweep capped at 30 GeV and 3 points
- Use particle_energy: [1, 10, 30] to run all momenta in parallel on GPU

Simulate muon tracks and MEASURE:
1. Energy deposit per layer
2. Hit detection efficiency
3. Material budget contribution

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Use mu- particles in Geant4 particle gun
- Test at most 3 configurations exploring topology and/or layer parameters

steps.name: generate_geometry_thin_layers_4
steps.description: Generate silicon pixel tracking detector geometry with 4 thin layers (100μm silicon, 15mm gaps)

steps.name: generate_geometry_medium_layers_6
steps.description: Generate silicon pixel tracking detector geometry with 6 medium layers (200μm silicon, 20mm gaps)

steps.name: generate_geometry_thick_layers_3
steps.description: Generate silicon pixel tracking detector geometry with 3 thick layers (300μm silicon, 25mm gaps)

steps.name: simulate_thin_layers_4
steps.description: Simulate muon tracks through thin 4-layer silicon detector at 1, 10, 30 GeV energies

steps.name: simulate_medium_layers_6
steps.description: Simulate muon tracks through medium 6-layer silicon detector at 1, 10, 30 GeV energies

steps.name: simulate_thick_layers_3
steps.description: Simulate muon tracks through thick 3-layer silicon detector at 1, 10, 30 GeV energies

steps.name: analyze_thin_layers_4
steps.description: Analyze tracking performance for thin 4-layer configuration: energy deposit per layer, hit efficiency, material budget

steps.name: analyze_medium_layers_6
steps.description: Analyze tracking performance for medium 6-layer configuration: energy deposit per layer, hit efficiency, material budget

steps.name: analyze_thick_layers_3
steps.description: Analyze tracking performance for thick 3-layer configuration: energy deposit per layer, hit efficiency, material budget

steps.name: optimization_comparison
steps.description: Compare all three configurations to identify optimal balance between momentum resolution and material budget

steps.name: detector_report
steps.description: Generate comprehensive detector design report with performance evaluation and recommendations
